description: Lacking a sharp edge or point; not keen or acute. 'Shoru' names the quality of dullness—edges that don't cut, points that don't pierce.
sound_symbolism: The word slides past. 'Sho' /ʃo/ begins with soft pressure, edge absent. Then 'ru' rolls without cutting, dullness confirmed.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'shoru kiru' (dull chisel). Opposite of 'tiso' (sharp). Can describe physical bluntness or mental sluggishness.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Dull awareness misses subtlety—the unsharpened mind overlooks what's present.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Dull tools taught maintenance—the consequence of neglect reminded craftspeople to care for their instruments.
